steps.title: Identify the key context clue
steps.content: The fog is "obliterating" something. Fog is a physical phenomenon that obscures — it erases visible things. So "traces" must be something observable that fog can eliminate.

steps.title: Select the word for visible marks
steps.content:
The word "traces" needs to mean visible signs or marks — the fog obliterated ______ of familiar landscapes. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Debris: obliterating debris of familiar landscapes — debris means scattered fragments or wreckage; the fog doesn't remove physical rubble, it obscures what can be seen. ✗
  • Choice B — Copies: obliterating copies of familiar landscapes — copies means reproductions or duplicates; the passage describes actual landmarks (the bridge), not copies of them. ✗
  • Choice C — Indications: obliterating indications of familiar landscapes — indications means visible signs or evidence of something, which is exactly what fog erases by obscuring outlines like the bridge against the sky. ✓
  • Choice D — Remembrances: obliterating remembrances of familiar landscapes — remembrances means memories; fog physically obscures what is visible, it does not erase memories. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
